title: viola palmata var. villosa
intro: Viola palmata var. villosa, also known as the hairy violet, is a plant species within the viola genus, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The hairy violet is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's health benefits include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, as well as its use as a topical treatment for skin irritations and wounds. Hairy violet is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
